summary: Converted most of Young Link's ftclink assembly into C, removing 529 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.s and adding src/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.c plus a new ftclink.h. The matched C covers ftCLink_OnDeath, ftCLink_OnLoad, item/held-item visibility helpers, callback setup, state checks, and cleanup/accessor routines around Fighter fields such as x1974_heldItem, x221B_flag, x2244, and x2200_ftcmd_var1. The PR also expanded Fighter and ftCommonData layout knowledge and wired ftclink.o into both e_files.mk and obj_files.mk. The PR body explicitly notes that some functions still remained in ftclink.s, so this was a partial rather than complete file decompilation.
what_changed: Removed most of the existing ftclink assembly block from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.s, including ftCLink_OnDeath, ftCLink_OnLoad, func_80148DC0 through func_80149318-era routines shown in the diff excerpt.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.c with C implementations for Young Link lifecycle and helper routines, including ftCLink_OnDeath, ftCLink_OnLoad, func_80148F38, func_80149018, func_80149114, func_8014919C, func_8014920C, func_80149268, func_801492C4, func_801492F4, and func_80149318.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.h with a CLinkAttributes struct using offset-based padding for fields at xC, x10, x2C, x48, x54, xBC, xC4, and xD8, plus prototypes for func_80148F38 and func_80149018 and ITEM_CLINK_MILK defined as 0x7B.

Updated Fighter and ftCommonData struct layouts in src/melee/ft/fighter.h to expose fields needed by the matched code, including ftCommonData.x294, Fighter.x4_index, xEC, x5F8, x19A4, x19A8, x19AC, and x221B_flag.
Added or adjusted declarations in include/functions.h and include/variables.h, including func_80092ED8 and extern s32** lbl_804D6540.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.o to e_files.mk and obj_files.mk so the new C translation unit participates in builds.
smart_moves: Kept the work scoped as a partial decompilation: the PR title and body both say 'most' and 'Still some remaining functions in ftclink.s', avoiding overclaiming full ftclink completion.
Introduced CLinkAttributes with explicit offset padding rather than guessing semantic field names, preserving layout correctness while allowing ftCLink_OnLoad and callback code to type-check.
Used named constants where evidence allowed it, notably ITEM_CLINK_MILK for item ID 0x7B, while leaving less-understood routines and fields as func_/x-offset names.

Matched branch-heavy held-item behavior as direct C control flow: func_80148F38 maps func_8026B320 return values 1 through 4 to func_80070FB4 argument values, preserving the assembly switch-like shape.

Exposed only the Fighter fields required by the new code while retaining filler arrays for unknown struct regions, which is a low-risk way to advance matching without prematurely naming unknown layout.
Preserved odd-looking assembly artifacts where needed for matching, such as repeated null checks/returns in func_80149268 and func_801492C4 and local unused variables in several functions.
decomp_lessons: For character files, moving matched functions out of asm requires synchronized changes across the C source, character header, shared Fighter layout, function/variable declarations, and build object lists.
When special attribute data is only partially understood, an offset-padded character-specific attributes struct is preferable to anonymous pointer arithmetic in every function.
Held-item and article logic often depends on helper calls with numeric return codes; preserve the observed comparisons and switch cases before attempting semantic renames.
Bitfield changes can be represented through existing flag structs when the assembly uses rlwimi on a byte, as shown by mapping x221B byte operations to x221B_flag.bits.b2/b3/b4.
Decompilation may require adding global/common data fields, such as ftCommonData.x294, because character-specific code may multiply by global common parameters.
Partial matches are still valuable if the remaining assembly is explicitly documented and the build is updated to compile the new object.
naming_conventions: Character lifecycle functions use the ftCLink_ prefix for known Young Link callbacks, e.g. ftCLink_OnDeath and ftCLink_OnLoad.
Unknown functions remain address-based func_80148xxx names rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
Unknown struct fields retain offset-based xNNN names, e.g. x1974_heldItem, x2200_ftcmd_var1, x2244, and CLinkAttributes.xD8.
Character attributes are named CLinkAttributes and stored in a dedicated ftclink.h header.
Item ID 0x7B was promoted to the named macro ITEM_CLINK_MILK, giving one semantic constant without renaming unrelated unknown item helpers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used offset-padded structs to align C field accesses with assembly offsets in both Fighter and CLinkAttributes.
Kept unused local variables in several routines, likely to reproduce stack frame/register allocation for matching.
Represented assembly branch ladders as straightforward null-guard returns in func_8014920C and cleanup routines, preserving early-return behavior.
Translated the assembly's case ladder in func_80148F38 into a C switch over func_8026B320 with cases 1, 2, 3, and 4.
Used explicit float constants 3.0f and 0.0f in func_8014908C and func_801490D0 corresponding to small data float loads in the removed assembly.
Preserved callback registration behavior by passing func_80149114 into func_8007B1B8 with a CLinkAttributes field address at xC4.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the remaining functions from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.s.
Search for later semantic renames of func_80148F38, func_80149018, func_80149114, and func_8014919C.
Check later Fighter struct updates for renamed fields x19A4, x19AC, x221B_flag, x2244, and x2200_ftcmd_var1.
Look for later cleanup of CLinkAttributes fields and ITEM_CLINK_MILK usage.
Search for review or later commits involving func_80092ED8 and lbl_804D6540 declarations.
